ApprovalLogOut.description: Approval log entry in request detail responses.
ApprovalLogOut.title: ApprovalLogOut

AutomationResultIn.description:
Input schema for automated equipment result submission.

Same simplification as the manual record_result path — server
randomises per-wafer verdict; the equipment integration only needs
to identify the dispatch and optionally attach a comment string.
AutomationResultIn.title: AutomationResultIn

CapabilityOut.description: Nested output for an equipment capability (experiment type summary).
CapabilityOut.title: CapabilityOut

CapabilitySetIn.description: Input schema for setting equipment capabilities.
CapabilitySetIn.title: CapabilitySetIn

CommentIn.description: Input for actions requiring a comment (return, reject).
CommentIn.title: CommentIn

DateRangeOut.description: Date range nested in request statistics responses.
DateRangeOut.title: DateRangeOut

DispatchBriefOut.description: Brief dispatch info nested in WIP responses.
DispatchBriefOut.title: DispatchBriefOut

DispatchDetailOut.description: Output schema for dispatch detail responses.
DispatchDetailOut.title: DispatchDetailOut

DispatchIn.description:
Input schema for creating a dispatch.

Chat-design: experiment_type is derived from the parent WIP; the
payload only carries the per-dispatch choices (equipment + recipe).
estimated_duration_seconds is optional — operators can leave it
blank, and the SPA falls back to a hardcoded 24h countdown.
Seconds (not minutes) so demo scenarios can specify sub-minute runs.
DispatchIn.title: DispatchIn

DispatchListOut.description: Output schema for dispatch list responses.
DispatchListOut.title: DispatchListOut

EquipmentBriefOut.description: Brief equipment info nested in utilization responses.
EquipmentBriefOut.title: EquipmentBriefOut

EquipmentUpdate.description: Input schema for partially updating equipment.
EquipmentUpdate.title: EquipmentUpdate

EquipmentUtilizationOut.description: Output schema for the equipment-utilization endpoint.
EquipmentUtilizationOut.title: EquipmentUtilizationOut

ExceptionReportIn.description: Input schema for reporting a dispatch exception.
ExceptionReportIn.title: ExceptionReportIn

ExperimentResultBrief.description: Result summary nested in the sample experiments rollup.
ExperimentResultBrief.title: ExperimentResultBrief

ExperimentResultOut.description:
Experiment result details nested in dispatch responses.

Pass/fail verdict lives per-wafer on SampleExperimentStatus.verdict
(exposed via SampleExperimentStatusOut + the sample-experiments
rollup). This block only carries the dispatch-level operator
comment.
ExperimentResultOut.title: ExperimentResultOut

ExperimentTypeBrief.description: Experiment type summary nested in the rollup.
ExperimentTypeBrief.title: ExperimentTypeBrief

ExperimentTypeIn.description: Input schema for creating an experiment type.
ExperimentTypeIn.title: ExperimentTypeIn

ExperimentTypeOut.description: Output schema for experiment type responses.
ExperimentTypeOut.title: ExperimentTypeOut

ExperimentTypeUpdate.description: Input schema for partially updating an experiment type.
ExperimentTypeUpdate.title: ExperimentTypeUpdate

ExperimentTypeWithParamsOut.description: Nested experiment type with per-request parameters.
ExperimentTypeWithParamsOut.title: ExperimentTypeWithParamsOut

LoginIn.description: Input schema for the login endpoint.
LoginIn.title: LoginIn

ReasonIn.description: Input for cancel action requiring a reason.
ReasonIn.title: ReasonIn

ReasonOptionalIn.description: Input for actions with optional reason (reject-receiving, report-lost, void, return sample).
ReasonOptionalIn.title: ReasonOptionalIn

RecipeExperimentTypeOut.description: Nested experiment type summary for recipe responses.
RecipeExperimentTypeOut.title: RecipeExperimentTypeOut

RecipeIn.description:
Input schema for creating a recipe.

Chat-design: no equipment_id — recipes are equipment-agnostic.
RecipeIn.title: RecipeIn

RecipeUpdate.description:
Input schema for partially updating a recipe.

All fields are optional. Omit a field to leave it unchanged.
RecipeUpdate.title: RecipeUpdate

RecordResultIn.description:
Input schema for recording a dispatch result.

Server randomises the per-wafer verdict (80% pass / 20% fail) — the
client never sends one. extra="forbid" so stale clients still
posting verdict/data/summary fail loudly (422) instead of silently
losing those fields' intent.
RecordResultIn.title: RecordResultIn

RefreshIn.description: Input schema for the token refresh endpoint.
RefreshIn.title: RefreshIn

RefreshOut.description: Output schema for the token refresh response.
RefreshOut.title: RefreshOut

RequestDetailOut.description: Output schema for request detail responses.
RequestDetailOut.title: RequestDetailOut

RequestIn.description: Input schema for creating a commission request.
RequestIn.title: RequestIn

RequestListOut.description: Output schema for request list responses.
RequestListOut.title: RequestListOut

RequestStatisticsOut.description: Output schema for the request-statistics endpoint.
RequestStatisticsOut.title: RequestStatisticsOut

RequestSummaryOut.description: Brief request summary nested in sample responses.
RequestSummaryOut.title: RequestSummaryOut

RequestUpdateIn.description:
Input schema for updating a draft/returned request.

title/note/urgency are partial-update fields that work on both
DRAFT and RETURNED requests. samples and experiment_type_ids are
DRAFT-only — reviewing a request locks the sample + experiment
set. None means "don't change this field"; an empty list is a
business-rule violation (samples need ≥1) and is rejected with
422 by update_request rather than slipping past as a no-op.
RequestUpdateIn.title: RequestUpdateIn

SampleBriefOut.description: Brief sample info nested in WIP responses.
SampleBriefOut.title: SampleBriefOut

SampleDetailOut.description: Output schema for sample detail responses.
SampleDetailOut.title: SampleDetailOut

SampleExperimentRollupOut.description:
One row of the per-sample experiments rollup.

Status semantics (computed server-side from the sample's dispatches):
  - "done": the latest dispatch for this experiment_type is COMPLETED
  - "in_progress": at least one non-terminal dispatch exists
  - "pending": no dispatch has been created yet
SampleExperimentRollupOut.title: SampleExperimentRollupOut

SampleExperimentStatusOut.description:
Output schema for per-sample experiment progress.

verdict is null until the dispatch's record_result fills it in
(random pass/fail per wafer).
SampleExperimentStatusOut.title: SampleExperimentStatusOut

SampleIn.description: Input for a single sample when creating a request.
SampleIn.title: SampleIn

SampleListOut.description: Output schema for sample list responses.
SampleListOut.title: SampleListOut

TokenOut.description: Output schema for login response with JWT tokens and user data.
TokenOut.title: TokenOut

TrendPointOut.description: One day's count on a trend series.
TrendPointOut.title: TrendPointOut

TrendsOut.description: Output schema for the trends endpoint.
TrendsOut.title: TrendsOut

UserOut.description: Output schema representing an authenticated user with role information.
UserOut.title: UserOut

WIPAddSamplesIn.description: Input schema for adding samples to an existing WIP.
WIPAddSamplesIn.title: WIPAddSamplesIn

WIPDetailOut.description: Output schema for WIP detail responses.
WIPDetailOut.title: WIPDetailOut

WIPIn.description:
Input schema for creating a WIP.

Chat-design: a WIP is bound to one experiment_type at creation time.
Equipment is chosen later, per dispatch.
WIPIn.title: WIPIn

WIPListOut.description: Output schema for WIP list responses.
WIPListOut.title: WIPListOut

post.description:
Authenticate a user and return JWT tokens.

Returns access token, refresh token, and user information on success,
or 401 on invalid credentials.
Revoke a refresh token. Requires authentication.

get.description: Return the currently authenticated user's information.

post.description:
Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access + refresh token pair.

Implements refresh token rotation: the old token is deleted and a new
one is created. This limits the window of abuse if a token is leaked.

Automated equipment result submission. Completes dispatch and creates result.

Accepts dispatch in DISPATCHED or RUNNING state. Runs the abbreviated
state machine chain: unload → record_result (which now lands
directly in COMPLETED).

get.description:
List dispatches with optional filters. Lab staff and managers only.

Selects created_by__profile so the response can include the
operator's username and department for each row without an N+1.
Get dispatch detail. Lab staff and managers only.

post.description:
Abort a dispatch. Lab staff and managers allowed.

Aborting clears one active dispatch — if the WIP had a prior
COMPLETED dispatch and this abort closes out the last in-flight
one, the WIP auto-completes via the same gate as record_result.
An all-aborted WIP (no COMPLETED at all) is intentionally NOT
auto-completed; the operator decides whether to manually complete
or open a fresh dispatch.

Record result for an unloaded dispatch — terminal step.

Payload is just {comment}. Per-wafer pass/fail verdicts are rolled
at unload time (see update_experiment_statuses_on_unload), so by
the time the Record Result modal opens, the operator can already
see the outcomes. This endpoint only persists the comment, lands
the dispatch in COMPLETED, stamps completed_at, and triggers the
WIP-level auto-complete cascade. Lab staff only.
Redispatch an exception dispatch. Creates a new PENDING dispatch. Lab staff only.
Report an execution exception. Lab staff only.
Start a dispatch (pending/dispatched → running). Lab staff only.

Unload a dispatch (dispatched/running → unloaded). Lab staff only.

Rolls the per-wafer pass/fail verdict on every SampleExperimentStatus
row for this dispatch's WIP × experiment_type. This happens at unload
time (not at record_result) so the Record Result modal can display
the outcomes before the operator finalises the run.

get.description: List equipment with optional search and filters.

post.description: Create a new equipment. Only lab staff and managers allowed.

get.description: Get a single equipment by ID.

patch.description: Partially update equipment. Only lab staff and managers allowed.

post.description: Replace all capabilities for an equipment.

get.description:
List experiment types with optional search and filters.

By default only active items are returned.

post.description: Create a new experiment type. Only lab staff and managers allowed.

delete.description: Soft-delete an experiment type. Only lab staff and managers allowed.

get.description: Get a single experiment type by ID.

patch.description: Partially update an experiment type. Only lab staff and managers allowed.

get.description: Return the health status of the API.
List recipes with optional filters.

post.description: Create a new recipe. Only lab staff and managers allowed.

delete.description: Soft-delete a recipe. Only lab staff and managers allowed.

get.description: Get a single recipe by ID.

patch.description: Partially update a recipe. Only lab staff and managers allowed.

get.description: Return equipment utilization statistics. Lab Manager only.
Return request statistics for a date range. Lab Manager only.

Return a per-day count series for the requested metric.

INTEGRATION_GAPS §4: backs the lab manager dashboard trend chart.
Currently only ``requests_per_day`` (Request rows grouped by
created_at::date) is supported; the metric set is centralised in
_TREND_METRICS so adding new series is one-line.

Zero-fills days with no rows so the SPA can plot a continuous
series of length ``days`` without client-side gap filling.
List commission requests. Fab users see only their own.

post.description: Create a new commission request (draft). Only fab users allowed.

delete.description: Hard-delete a draft request. Only the owning fab user may do this.

get.description: Get request detail with samples, experiment types, and approval logs.

patch.description:
Update a draft or returned request. Only the requester (fab user) allowed.

Scalar fields (title/note/urgency) can be patched on DRAFT and
RETURNED. samples and experiment_type_ids are draft-only — once a
reviewer has seen the request, the sample set and required
experiments are locked, even after a return. INTEGRATION_GAPS §2.9.

post.description: Approve a pending request. Only lab managers allowed.
Cancel a request. Fab user can cancel own; lab manager can cancel any.
Close a completed request. Only lab managers allowed.
Reject a pending request with comment. Only lab managers allowed.
Return a pending request with comment. Only lab managers allowed.
Mark an approved request as shipped. Only the requester allowed.
Submit a draft/returned request for approval. Only the requester allowed.

get.description:
List samples with optional filters. Fab users see only their own.

Annotates ``has_wip`` (True when the sample is in at least one
non-terminal WIP) so the SPA's Lab Samples page can derive its
``in_wip`` pill without a second round-trip — see
INTEGRATION_GAPS §3.2 (sample status enum) and §4.
Get sample detail with request info.
Get experiment progress for a sample. Fab users see only their own samples.

Per-experiment-type rollup for one sample's wafer detail page.

For each experiment_type required by the sample's parent request,
inspects dispatches across this sample's WIPs and returns the
status, dispatch_id, and (when done) the result. INTEGRATION_GAPS
§2.8 resolution A — saves the SPA three extra requests per wafer
detail open.

post.description:
Confirm sample receipt. Only lab staff/managers allowed.

Stamps Sample.received_at with the current time on the receive
transition. The frontend uses this to render the urgency countdown
on the Samples list (deadline = received_at + urgency_duration),
so it must be captured at the moment of receipt, not derived from
later updated_at values.
Mark sample as receiving exception. Only lab staff/managers allowed.
Mark sample as lost. Only lab staff/managers allowed.
Return a sample (from exception states). Only lab staff/managers allowed.
Void a sample (from exception/lost states). Only lab staff/managers allowed.

get.description: List WIPs. Lab staff and managers only.

post.description:
Create a WIP for one experiment_type with the given samples.

Chat-design: equipment is chosen later per-dispatch; WIP only pins
the experiment_type and the sample batch.

get.description: Get WIP detail with dispatches. Lab staff and managers only.

post.description: Abort a WIP. Marks associated samples as processing_exception.

Complete a WIP. Requires all dispatches to be in terminal state.

Auto-completes samples and checks if parent requests are done.

Create a dispatch for a WIP using the chosen equipment + recipe.

Chat-design: experiment_type is derived from the parent WIP, so the
payload only carries equipment_id, recipe_id, and an optional note.
Validates that the equipment supports the WIP's experiment_type, the
recipe targets the same experiment_type, and the equipment has room.

Rejects with 422 if the WIP already has an active dispatch (anything
other than COMPLETED or ABORTED). A WIP can accumulate many
dispatches over its lifetime, but only one at a time.

Automatically transitions WIP to in_progress on first dispatch.

Add samples to an existing WIP. Lab staff only.

The new samples' parent requests must include the WIP's experiment_type.
